items.q: Which of the 6 Rs has the GREATEST environmental benefit?
items.correct: Refuse or Reduce (preventing the impact at source).
items.why: Students treat recycling as the gold standard, but recycling uses energy and downgrades materials. The hierarchy is Refuse, Reduce, Reuse, Repair, Recycle, Rethink - PREVENTION beats reprocessing.

items.q: What is the purpose of a jig in manufacturing?
items.correct: To guide a tool and ensure consistent, accurate repeated operations.
items.misconception: To hold the workpiece very tightly so it does not move.
items.why: Students confuse jigs with fixtures. A fixture HOLDS the workpiece; a jig GUIDES the tool (e.g., a drill jig guides the drill bit to the correct position) - though the terms are sometimes used loosely.

items.correct: A drawing showing multiple 2D views (front, side, plan) of an object.
items.misconception: A 3D drawing showing the object at an angle.
items.random_wrong: A drawing in black ink only.
items.why: Students confuse orthographic (multi-view 2D) with isometric (single 3D view). Orthographic projects each face flat onto separate views - used for engineering specification.
It's Thermoplastic. “Thermoset” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Heat. “Light” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many smart material examples are listed in this lesson?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
